content.title: Introduction
content.definition: Though you may approach a course in anatomy and physiology strictly as a requirement for your field of study, the knowledge you gain in this course will serve you well in many aspects of your life.

content.title: 1.1 Overview of Anatomy and Physiology
content.definition: Human anatomy is the scientific study of the body’s structures.
content.summary: Human anatomy is the scientific study of the body’s structures. Some of these structures are very small and can only be observed and analyzed with the assistance of a microscope. Other larger structures can readily be seen, manipulated, measured, and weighed. The word “anatomy...

content.title: 1.1 Overview of Anatomy and Physiology
content.definition: Anatomists take two general approaches to the study of the body’s structures: regional and systemic.
content.summary: Anatomists take two general approaches to the study of the body’s structures: regional and systemic. Regional anatomy is the study of the interrelationships of all of the structures in a specific body region, such as the abdomen. Studying regional anatomy helps us appreciate t...

content.title: 1.2 Structural Organization of the Human Body
content.definition: Before you begin to study the different structures and functions of the human body, it is helpful to consider its basic architecture; that is, how its smallest parts are assembled into larger structures.

hierarchy.category: 1.2 Structural Organization of the Human Body

content.title: The Levels of Organization
content.definition: To study the chemical level of organization, scientists consider the simplest building blocks of matter: subatomic particles, atoms and molecules.
content.summary: To study the chemical level of organization, scientists consider the simplest building blocks of matter: subatomic particles, atoms and molecules. All matter in the universe is composed of one or more unique pure substances called elements, familiar examples of which are hydro...

hierarchy.category: 1.2 Structural Organization of the Human Body

content.title: The Levels of Organization
content.definition: The organism level is the highest level of organization.
content.summary: The organism level is the highest level of organization. An organism is a living being that has a cellular structure and that can independently perform all physiologic functions necessary for life. In multicellular organisms, including humans, all cells, tissues, organs, and o...
